title: Scabiosa macedonica
intro: Scabiosa macedonica, commonly known as the Macedonian scabious, is a flowering plant species in the genus Scabiosa, known for its delicate, sky-blue or pink flowers and slender stems. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential remedy for various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Scabiosa macedonica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make teas, infusions, and topical preparations, and is also used in some herbal supplements to support immune function and skin health.
taxonomy: Scabiosa macedonica is classified in the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically within the family Dipsacaceae. It belongs to the genus Scabiosa, which includes a variety of species commonly known as pincushion flowers. The species Scabiosa macedonica is native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Eastern Europe. It is a flowering plant that is often cultivated for its vibrant blue or white blooms. Further classification includes the order Dipsacales, and the class Magnoliopsida.
